## Description

Resources for writing internal communications using company-specific formats. Covers status reports, leadership updates, newsletters, FAQs, incident reports, and project updates.

## When to Use

- Writing status reports
- Creating leadership updates
- Drafting company newsletters
- Writing incident reports
- Creating FAQs
- Project updates and announcements
- Internal memos and communications

## When NOT to Use

- External communications
- Customer-facing content
- Public documentation
- Social media posts

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Identify Communication Type

- Determine audience and purpose
- Review company communication guidelines
- Gather required information

### Phase 2: Structure Content

- Follow company format/template
- Organize information logically
- Include required sections

### Phase 3: Draft & Review

- Write content
- Review for clarity and tone
- Get feedback from stakeholders

### Phase 4: Finalize & Distribute

- Polish language and formatting
- Verify accuracy
- Distribute through proper channels

## Tools & References

- **Related Skills**: writing-clearly-and-concisely
- **Templates**: Company-specific formats
- **Distribution**: Internal channels and systems

## Best Practices

- Follow company communication guidelines
- Be clear and concise
- Include relevant metrics or data
- Proofread carefully
- Get appropriate approvals
- Archive communications
- Maintain consistent tone
